signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + US Labor Strength
signals.text: USD 1W outperformance (+1.328%) against the NZD laggard (-1.668%) creates a 2.996% G8 spread following the robust US NFP report. The 150bp USD/NZD carry advantage and NZD's -0.83% real rate disadvantage accelerate the plunge toward 0.5790 as risk assets tumble. The next Fed meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 17 Jun determines if this hawkish USD repricing sustains the breakdown.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Demand Slump + Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: WTI crude's -3.00% drop to $90.2 on weak China demand and Iran peace hopes drives USD/CAD toward 1.3933. CAD COT net short -42.1k aligns with the fundamental weakness, though the 87% retail short position warns of a contrarian squeeze if oil stabilizes. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 10 Jun is the catalyst for resolving the CAD's 137bp OIS carry disadvantage.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD COT net long +58.041k (76% long) faces liquidation risk as the pair closed Friday at 0.7050 following the USD rally. Negative AUD economic surprises (CESI net -8) and a 74bp OIS carry disadvantage against the USD undermine the RBA's restrictive stance. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 16 Jun determines if the hold bias can withstand the deteriorating data trend.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry Advantage vs Speculative Squeeze Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY closed at 160.293, supported by a 288bp OIS carry advantage and a bear-steepening US yield curve (2s10s +55.6bp). JPY COT net short -86.2k creates significant squeeze risk if elevated VIX (21.5) triggers a broader risk-off deleveraging. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 97% hike priced) on 16 Jun is the catalyst for any shift in the JPY's -0.69% real rate disadvantage.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hike Bias vs USD Data Dominance
signals.text: EUR/USD downside toward 1.1527 is reinforced by a 168bp OIS carry disadvantage and strong USD economic surprises (CESI +29). While the ECB maintains a hike bias, the EUR's -0.26% real rate remains accommodative compared to the USD's +1.23% restrictive stance. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hike priced) on 11 Jun determines if hawkish rhetoric can offset the 1.75-month high in the DXY.
